mnemonics: TEST: Trees, Efficiency, Structures, Traversals - Remember the core components for a test on this module.
keyPoints: Understanding and implementing tree-based data structures (e.g., Binary Search Trees, AVL Trees, B-Trees)
Analyzing time and space complexity (Big-O) of algorithms on these structures
Mastering different tree traversal techniques (In-order, Pre-order, Post-order, Level-order)
commonMistakes: Confusing the properties and use cases of different tree types (e.g., using a BST where a B-Tree is more appropriate)
Incorrectly calculating the time complexity for operations, especially for balanced vs. unbalanced trees
Off-by-one errors or incorrect pointer/node handling during tree traversal and manipulation
